per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've noticed that when the tightness happens, I start sweating a lot and sometimes get a bit of nausea, but it's never been severe enough to make me vomit. And just recently, during one of the more intense episodes, my left arm started tingling, which really freaked me out.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've noticed that when the chest tightness happens, I start sweating a lot. It's like my body is trying to cool down or something, and it's really unsettling to feel all this sweat on me while I'm already anxious about what's going on with my heart.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yes, I've also noticed some swelling in my ankles lately. My shoes feel tighter than usual, which is why I'm a bit concerned about fluid retention or something along those lines.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My father actually had a heart attack at age 52 and passed away from it. It was a real wake-up call for me, especially since we were close. But my mom is still doing okay, she's got hypertension and type 2 diabetes like I do, but she's managing it pretty well at 78 years old.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, it definitely has made me more aware of my own health and the importance of taking care of myself. Every time I experience chest tightness or any other symptoms, I can't help but think about my father's heart attack and wonder if something similar could happen to me. It's a constant worry in the back of my mind, especially now that I'm getting older too.
